titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 608

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=11 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.265948+00 | translation moa=12 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-29 15:57:28.253279+00

div.p:
W. Rex Scott’ Iusticiis . Vicecomitibus . prepositis . Ministris . Cuntisque aliis probis hominibus tocius terre sue salutem . Sci
atis me concessisse deo et Sancto cuthberto de Goldingcham et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus . ut ipsi et eorum
seruientes per totam terram meam sint liberi et quieti ab omni theloneo de propriis catallis et mercaturis
eorum . prohibeo itaque ut nemo contra hanc libertatem eis infra terram meam uim aut iniuriam faciat super meam forisfacturam sicut carta Regis M. fratris mei testatur et confirmat . t’ . Engeramo episcopo de Glasgu . Nicolao cancellario . Waltero filio alani . Apud Striuelin .

William, king of Scots, to the justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all other worthy men of the whole of his land, greeting: Know that I have granted to God and to Saint Cuthbert at Coldingham and to the monks serving God there, that they and their servants shall be free and quit throughout the whole of my land from all toll on their own chattels and merchandise. I therefore forbid that anyone shall cause violence or injury to them against this liberty within my land upon pain of my forfeiture, just as the charter of King Malcolm my brother bears witness and confirms. As witnesses: Ingram the bishop of Glasgow, Nicholas the chancellor, Walter son of Alan. At Stirling.
